title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: There are many known risk factors for heart disease. Risk factors you can control include high blood cholesterol and triglycerides, high blood pressure, diabetes, pre-diabetes, being overweight or obese. Smoking, lack of physical activity, an unhealthy diet, and stress.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: Risk factors you can't control include age, gender, and having a family history of heart

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: If you already have heart disease and multiple or severe risk factors, your doctor may lower your LDL goal to less than 70. Your specific goals may vary depending on your health situation. Risk your doctor what your lipid goals should be.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: A healthcare professional can measure your blood pressure to find out if you have high blood pressure. This video will help you understand what the numbers in your blood pressure mean. Your blood pressure can be measured with a blood pressure cuff called a sphigmomanometer.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: When your heart beats, the pressure of blood on the walls of your arteries is called systolic pressure.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: When your heart relaxes between beats, pressure on the artery wall is called diastolic pressure.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: Your blood pressure may change throughout the day. However, optimal systolic pressure should be less than 120 and optimal diastolic pressure should be less than 80.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: If your systolic pressure frequently stays above 130 or your diastolic pressure frequently stays above 80, you have high blood pressure.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: Your specific goals may vary depending on your health situation. Ask your doctor what your blood pressure goals should be.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: This video will help you understand how blood pressure is controlled in your body. Blood pressure is the amount of force caused by blood pressing against the walls of your arteries. It is tightly controlled to ensure sufficient blood reaches all parts of your body.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: Arteries are blood vessels that carry blood away from your heart to your tissues. This blood supplies your tissues with the oxygen and nutrients they need to function.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: In your heart there are two lower chambers called ventricles which contract with each heart beat. The right ventricle pushes oxygen poor blood to your lungs to pick up oxygen and the left ventricle pushes oxygen rich blood through your arteries to your body.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: As blood flows through your arteries four main factors affect the pressure on the artery walls.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: The first is the amount of blood your ventricles push out of your heart each minute, also known as cardiac output. Your blood pressure goes up as cardiac output increases.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: The second factor affecting your blood pressure is the total amount of blood in your body or blood volume. Blood pressure also goes up as blood volume increases.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: The third factor that affects your blood pressure is resistance. Resistance is anything working against the blood flow through your arteries. Resistance is mainly affected by the width of your arteries. Your body is able to increase the width of your arteries to lower your blood pressure. Or it can reduce the width to raise your blood pressure.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: The fourth factor that affects your blood pressure is viscosity. Viscosity is how thick your blood is. In your blood more particles such as proteins and fat increase viscosity. If your blood is thicker your blood pressure goes up as your heart works harder to push it through your arteries.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: If you have questions about high blood pressure or any medications you have been prescribed speak with your doctor. It is important to take your medications as directed and report any side effects you have.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If you or someone you know has high blood pressure, this video will help you understand what it is and why it's important to keep it under control.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Over time, high blood pressure will damage the walls of your arteries. This damage can lead to life-threatening conditions.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: For example, an artery wall may become weak and form an enlarged area called an aneurysm. The wall may burst and bleed into the tissue around it.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: In another example, damage to an artery wall may attract certain substances in your blood, such as cholesterol, fat and calcium. They may form a buildup called a plaque. As the plaque gets bigger, blood flow in the artery is reduced.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Blood cells can stick to the plaque and form solid clumps called clots. Blood clots further reduce or completely block your blood flow.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If this happens in your brain, it can lead to a stroke.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If it happens in your heart, it can lead to a heart attack.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: In another example, damage to the arteries in your kidneys may reduce their ability to work properly. This can lead to kidney disease.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: In a final example, damage to your arteries makes your heart beat even harder, which raises your blood pressure even more and can lead to heart failure.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If you have questions about high blood pressure or any medications you have been prescribed for it, speak with your doctor. It is important to take your medications as directed and report any side effects you have.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: If you or someone you know has high blood pressure, this video will show you some ways to manage it. In most cases, the cause of high blood pressure or hypertension is unknown. This type of high blood pressure is called primary or essential hypertension. Treatment for it includes lifestyle changes such as eating a healthy diet.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: If you are sensitive to the sodium insult, your doctor may tell you to eat less salt and processed foods. Sodium may cause your body to retain water. This increases the amount of your blood and makes your blood pressure go up.
